name: Crushed Tomatoes (with No Added Liquid)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the tomatoes and dip them in boiling water for 30 to 60 seconds or until the skins split.
2. Remove the tomatoes from the hot water and cool them quickly by placing them in cold water.
3. Trim off any bruised, discolored portions and cut into quarters.
4. Heat one-sixth of the tomato quarters quickly in a large pot, crushing them with a wooden mallet or spoon as they are added to the pot.
5. Continue heating the tomatoes, stirring occasionally until they reach boiling point.
6. Gradually add the remaining tomato quarters, crushing them as you go and stirring constantly.
7. Boil gently for 5 minutes after all the tomatoes have been added.
8. Add bottled lemon juice or citric acid to jars (see acidification directions).
9. Add 1 teaspoon of salt per quart to the jars, if desired.
10. Fill jars immediately with hot tomatoes, leaving a half-inch headspace.
11. Adjust lids and process as recommended.
Recommended processing times:
- Dial-gauge pressure canner
- Pints: 20 minutes at altitudes 0-2,000 ft; 6 minutes above 2,000-4,000 ft; 8 minutes above 4,000-6,000 ft; 9 minutes above 6,000-8,000 ft
- Quarts: 15 minutes at altitudes 0-2,000 ft; 11 minutes above 2,000-4,000 ft; 13 minutes above 4,000-6,000 ft; 14 minutes above 6,000-8,000 ft
- Weighted-gauge pressure canner
- Pints: 20 minutes at pressures of 5 PSI for altitudes up to 1,000 ft and 10 PSI above 1,000 ft.
- Quarts: 15 minutes at pressures of 10 PSI for altitudes up to 1,000 ft and 15 PSI above 1,000 ft.
- Boiling-water canner
- Pints: 35 minutes at altitudes up to 1,000 ft; 40 minutes between 1,001-3,000 ft; 45 minutes between 3,001-6,000 ft; and 50 minutes above 6,000 ft.
- Quarts: 45 minutes at altitudes up to 1,000 ft; 50 minutes between 1,001-3,000 ft; 55 minutes between 3,001-6,000 ft; and 60 minutes above 6,000 ft.
